title: Weekly Logistics Infrastructure Intelligence Report
executiveSummary: Terminal infrastructure continues to attract institutional capital, with the Stonepeak-CMA CGM platform still serving as the clearest hard-asset signal in the market.
Fresh U.S. federal port awards announced on March 26 reinforce that public capital remains an active accelerant for modernization and freight-corridor upgrades.
Private 5G deployments in UK port environments and freeport settings are increasingly being framed as operating infrastructure rather than innovation pilots.
Measured throughput gains at Port Saint John continue to give investors a live example of modernization translating into operating performance.
European congestion, Gulf-linked surcharges, and post-pilot AI execution risk keep resilience and implementation discipline central to underwriting.

marketSignals.summary: This week’s most relevant signals combine infrastructure capital, public-funding momentum, operating proof points, and continued freight-network fragility. For investors, the question is no longer whether logistics infrastructure remains attractive, but which assets and programs can convert capex into resilient performance.

rows.Headline: Stonepeak-CMA CGM terminal platform remains the benchmark capital signal
rows.Summary: Stonepeak is investing $2.4 billion for a 25% stake in United Ports LLC, the 10-terminal joint venture formed with CMA CGM.
rows.Why investors should pay attention: It continues to confirm institutional demand for scarce gateway assets with long-duration cash-flow potential.

rows.Headline: U.S. DOT announces nearly half a billion dollars in port investment
rows.Summary: The U.S. Department of Transportation announced nearly $489 million in port-related investment across 31 projects on March 26, 2026.
rows.Why investors should pay attention: Fresh awards move modernization from funding intent to actual deployment and create clearer local execution pipelines.

rows.Headline: Port Saint John continues to provide modernization proof
rows.Summary: DP World reported 29.4% container volume growth in 2025 at Port Saint John following the CAD$247 million modernization program.
rows.Why investors should pay attention: It offers a measurable benchmark for how port capex can translate into throughput and network relevance.

rows.Headline: New York port infrastructure solicitation remains active
rows.Summary: NYSERDA’s up to $300 million port and waterfront infrastructure program remains a visible state-level modernization signal.
rows.Why investors should pay attention: State funding windows continue to determine which projects become execution-ready first.

rows.Headline: Maersk flags continuing European network pressure in April update
rows.Summary: Maersk’s April Europe market update points to yard density, weather, and holiday-related pressure across North Europe and Mediterranean operations.
rows.Why investors should pay attention: Congestion and operational instability remain relevant to asset utilization, turnaround times, and resilience-oriented capex.

capitalFlow.summary: Capital continues to cluster around infrastructure-grade logistics assets, modernization programs backed by public funding, and network businesses with visible operating leverage. The notable pattern is that public and private capital are still reinforcing each other rather than substituting for one another.

rows.Strategic implication: Reinforces terminal platforms as core infrastructure allocation targets.
Fresh federal awards support project sequencing and sharpen visibility into near-term U.S. port execution.
Maintains federal support for port and freight-corridor capex.

rows.Investor or buyer: Government of Canada, Province of New Brunswick, Port Saint John, DP World
rows.Amount if known: CAD$247M project; CAD$75M+ DP World investment
rows.Strategic implication: Demonstrates funded modernization with measurable throughput and connectivity outcomes.
Public funding continues to accelerate infrastructure readiness and co-investment potential.

technologyDeployments.summary: Technology deployment now matters primarily as evidence of operational maturity. The strongest signals remain private 5G and AI systems being implemented in environments where reliability, automation, safety, and coordination directly affect asset performance.

rows.Operator or sponsor: Three Group Solutions / Hutchison Ports UK
rows.Operational impact: Supports autonomous vehicles, remote cranes, and other automation-heavy workflows.
Builds a connectivity layer for AI, automation, and real-time coordination across logistics sites.
Pushes AI into preventive safety and incident reduction in terminal operations.
Suggests deeper convergence of AI planning, scheduling, and terminal safety systems.

risks.summary: Disruption risk remains broad rather than isolated. For infrastructure investors, that matters because it affects terminal utilization, working-capital assumptions, labor planning, fuel pass-through, and the value of resilience-oriented upgrades.

rows.Event: Yard density, weather disruption, and Easter-related labor pressure
rows.Potential logistics impact: Longer dwell times, delayed cargo clearance, and schedule instability across European port networks.

rows.Region: Upper Gulf, Arabian Gulf, Persian Gulf
rows.Potential logistics impact: Higher freight costs and continuing uncertainty in routing economics for exposed trades.

rows.Event: Middle East disruption continues to pressure multimodal networks
rows.Potential logistics impact: Further pass-through into freight pricing, route variability, and planning complexity for trade-linked infrastructure.
Projects without permitting and execution readiness may miss near-term public-capital windows.

narratives.summary: The investor narrative is tightening around two ideas: first, capital still prefers infrastructure with hard operating relevance; second, technology must now prove that it can improve performance in live environments. That continues to support a PE and infrastructure-focused briefing rather than a VC-style innovation narrative.

rows.Narrative theme: Terminal infrastructure remains a preferred allocation
rows.Why investors care: Scarce gateway assets continue to attract large-scale institutional capital.

rows.Narrative theme: Public funding is still steering modernization timing
rows.Supporting signals: USDOT awards, NYSERDA, PIDP, Saint John
rows.Why investors care: Funding windows and public-private alignment are still driving execution order.

rows.Narrative theme: Private 5G is becoming a core operating layer
rows.Supporting signals: Hutchison UK and Thames Freeport deployments
rows.Why investors care: Connectivity is increasingly tied to automation and reliability, not experimentation.

rows.Narrative theme: AI diligence has shifted to post-pilot execution
rows.Supporting signals: Busan rollout, Shandong showcase, and pilot-to-scale critique
rows.Why investors care: Investors care whether AI survives integration into live operating systems.

rows.Narrative theme: Disruption resilience remains part of core underwriting
rows.Supporting signals: Maersk Europe update, Hapag-Lloyd war-risk pricing, FT shipping disruption coverage
rows.Why investors care: Freight instability continues to affect throughput, cost, and infrastructure value capture.

andrewLinkedInPost.title: From modernization momentum to operating resilience
andrewLinkedInPost.body: One of the clearest logistics infrastructure signals this week is that the market is rewarding operating proof, not just infrastructure intent. The latest U.S. federal port awards reinforce that public capital is still moving, but the more important question is where that capital is producing durable operating outcomes. Port Saint John remains a useful benchmark because modernization has already started to show up in throughput. At the same time, private 5G deployments in UK ports and continued pressure across European and Gulf-linked trade lanes are reinforcing the same point from another angle. Investors are not just backing infrastructure. They are backing infrastructure that can perform better under strain. That makes the diligence lens more practical than conceptual. The key question is less about who has the most technology and more about which assets, networks, and operating models are converting investment into resilient performance.

quaychainPost.title: This week’s infrastructure signal
quaychainPost.body: This week’s logistics infrastructure picture is straightforward: modernization funding is still moving, private 5G deployments are becoming more operational, and completed upgrades like Port Saint John are starting to show measurable throughput gains. At the same time, European network pressure and Gulf-linked surcharges continue to keep resilience central to underwriting. For investors and infrastructure advisors, the relevant filter is practical: which assets can convert capital into reliable operating performance under real-world pressure?

engagementTopics.summary: The strongest discussion areas this week are the ones where Andrew can add an infrastructure and execution lens rather than a vendor narrative.

rows.Why it matters: Investors are still discussing terminal valuation, control, and asset scarcity.
rows.Suggested comment: This continues to look like an asset-quality and strategic-location story, with institutional capital following infrastructure that is difficult to replicate.

rows.Topic: Port modernization with measurable throughput outcomes
rows.Why it matters: Saint John remains a useful operating benchmark after major modernization spend.
rows.Suggested comment: The strongest modernization signals are the ones that show up in volume growth, service reliability, and network relevance rather than in project language alone.

rows.Why it matters: Investors remain wary of deployments that do not survive production conditions.
rows.Suggested comment: Pilot success matters less than whether the deployment can integrate into workflows and continue producing value under live operating pressure.

communities.summary: Public LinkedIn communities and pages remain the most reliable channels for investor-adjacent visibility.

rows.Why relevant: Strong supply chain and infrastructure audience with analyst overlap.
Useful freight-market and pricing discussion with investor relevance.
Broad logistics audience that still captures capital-flow and modernization discussion.

outreachTargets.summary: These remain the most relevant investor and analyst targets for this week’s signal set.

rows.Name: James Wyper
rows.Why relevant: Closely tied to the lead infrastructure-capital transaction.

rows.Name: Lars Jensen
rows.Why relevant: High-signal perspective on shipping economics and network disruption.

rows.Name: Peter Sand
rows.Why relevant: Relevant for freight-rate, surcharge, and corridor-risk interpretation.

newsletterIdea.title: From funding awards to operating proof in logistics infrastructure
newsletterIdea.description: A weekly briefing connecting terminal capital, fresh federal awards, private 5G deployment, and live evidence of modernization translating into throughput and resilience.
newsletterIdea.whyInvestorsShouldRead: It keeps the focus on durable infrastructure outcomes and post-rollout execution quality rather than announcement flow.

actions: Engage in terminal JV, port modernization, and AI pilot-to-scale discussion threads.
Comment once in a LinkedIn community from the target list.
Publish a short X thread on why operating proof matters more than modernization announcements alone.
Send outreach to 2-3 investor and analyst targets with a message focused on durable operating performance.
